programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: With the help of his hero Ron Allum, Lars plans to dive to the bottom of the Mariana Trench in a submersible called the Deep Sea Challenger. Glitch plans to beat him there by sending a Grimbot in his Grimsub, but it can't withstand the heavy pressure of the water. Lars and Xuli must work together to mount a rescue operation at the bottom of the sea.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of Jeremy Fisher's Recital
programme.desc: When Sammy Whiskers and Mr Tod crash Jeremy Fisher's annual lake-side get together, it's up to Peter to save the party.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts follow a strange song to a lonely humpback whale who is alone because he doesn't sound like the other whales!

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: When Zigzag hurts her foot on the job, Vida is there to help fix it. However, Zigzag still has to deliver her packages in time for Vida's surprise Vet Day party.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Nori and Preston are creating a circle of standing stones, like the ancients of Swoppiton used to make. The stones are big - will it take more than Mojo's crane top to move them?

programme.title: Activate with Joe Wicks
programme.desc: Travel through time with my Time Warp Challenge. Are you ready to time jump? Jump through arm circles, runners and toe taps, then head back to the future for the Activate!

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Today, Mr Tumble is going on an adventure to the bottom of his garden. Meanwhile, Justin and his friends are having an adventurous day looking for animals, climbing and grass sledging!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bingo and Bluey race to the summit of Mt Mumandad, but between powerful storms, avalanches and stinky spots, this takes teamwork.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Mum sets up a backyard obstacle course just for fun, but Dad wants to race. Now, the kids are determined to win by any means necessary.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are struggling to build the Clubhouse out of building blocks. It keeps falling over! Duggee explains that the Squirrels need to be a bit of an Architect to create buildings that don't fall down.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels must walk back to the Clubhouse to get Duggee a bandage for his sore foot. Walking is so exhausting. Will they ever make it back? Not until the Squirrels meet a few of their friends on their way.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Nugget's new fitness device sparks a craze on the berg. Running, skating and jumping, the Piris battle it out to score the most points, with a surprise winner.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Dad drops an essential gizmo in a hard-to-reach place. Big Lizard takes them on an underwater adventure to meet Ammon, the tentacled solution to their problem.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger and the baby Pea-Rexes get caught in the middle of out-of-control rolling Coconutdons and tail-swatting Pineapplesaurus.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Ubercorn and Glitch head to the Svalbard Global Seed Vault, hoping to keep some seeds from Glitch's rare fruit tree safe for the future. But, just like when they were cadets together, the two of them get into trouble. Glitch accidentally crashes the Grimbler into the vault, and its hot engines start melting the ice that keeps the seeds cool. With the Go Jetters delayed by a malfunctioning Jet Pad, Ubercorn and Glitch try to save the scientists, the vault and Glitch's rare seeds.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasel teams up with the cheating Chipmunk to win a game of basketball.

programme.title: Spidey and His Amazing Friends
programme.sub-title: The Rhino and the Goose; Dog vs. Cat
programme.desc: Team Spidey helps a mama goose get back to her lost baby gosling. When Lockjaw causes trouble chasing Black Cat through the city, Team Spidey must help him make things right.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: The Octonauts expand their exploration beyond the sea and onto land. With new rides and new friends, they are prepared to protect any habitats and animals at risk on land or underwater.

programme.title: Stan Can
programme.desc: Stanley helps the kids play their big football match in all weather conditions, escalating into his latest creation, a high tech stadium!

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: In the perilous plant aisle, Supertato and Evil Pea team up to find treasure as they have half a map each, but she tricks him and steals the map to find the treasure for herself.

programme.title: Talking Tom Heroes: Suddenly Super
programme.desc: Ben upgrades his supersuit to make himself bolder and more fearless.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: In this doo wop number, the Potatoes dress up in special ways. They get ready for a party and just try on silly costumes.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: It's a very special day in the attic, but Rebecca seems to have forgotten all about it. Murphy has an inspirational idea for a 'bee-autiful' yogurt pot bee, the Mini Monsters transform paper plates into snappy-mouthed animals, and Rebecca gets a call from her uncle at the zoo. Finally, the Recycling Express inspires Murphy to help Rebecca turn different-sized cardboard boxes into a cake.

programme.title: Big Cook Little Cook
programme.desc: Small is pretending to be a detective as something has eaten all the salad in the garden. Wiggly Woo the worm comes to visit, Small flies off on her spoon to find out how rhubarb is harvested, and the two chefs cook a delicious worm crumble for their wiggly guest.

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to a great place to fly their paper planes. On the way they discover a secret squirrel den, sit on a dragonfly, race like boats and even walk like dinosaurs.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: PC Plum is determined to perform Great-Granny Plum's song at the Balamory ceilidh all by himself, but he soon realises it isn't easy being a one-man band.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee's nephew Dudley comes to stay. He is one cute puppy, but he looks like he could cause chaos in the clubhouse. And what's that terrible smell? The Squirrels come up with lots of imaginative ways to entertain him, but there is only one character that can stop him crying. Who could it be?

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: When hungry gulper eels as well as undersea tremors threaten Inkling and Kwazii, Dashi uses the new, multi-purpose Octo Max Suit to rescue them.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Tidbit gets a mosquito bite on picture day, and he doesn't want a photo. However, Vida has an idea.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters visit St Basil's Cathedral, but birthday boy Grandmaster Glitch has glitched it.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: It's raining, so Rafi and Ruby are playing indoor football. But when they accidentally break a plate, Rafi wishes for it to be fixed but warns them not to play again.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Stripes and Toad see a beautiful sunrise and decide to write a song about it together, but Stripes wants the song to be rocky, while Toad wants it to be slower. When they fall out and go off to write their own songs separately, Yukee tries to help them become friends again and create one song together.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's Grapefruit dedicating the song `All My Friends' to the close pals who go with her everywhere.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends are on a pirate adventure, searching for buried treasure. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is struggling to decide which story to read to Tumble Ted.

programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home
programme.desc: Gemma, Benjee Bear and Bailee Bear welcome toddlers Suuvi, Margot, Jasper, Sammie, Teddy and Tehlani, and their grown-ups, to the Toddler Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too. The `What's in the Box?' game reveals some wooden eggs, a toy chicken and a soft basket, and when the toddlers and parents open up a bigger box, they discover lots more objects and toys that are inspired by things that might be found on the farm.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Kiri and Lou are upset with each other but can't remember why.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: The Piggies build a castle so that Rebecca can be a knight and start an epic quest. But will everything go smoothly with Wilf as the dragon?

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Join sisters Eden and Ava as they become superheroes. First they create their characters `Nature Bunny Girl' and `H-Winter Girl', not forgetting new personas for Mum and Dad too.

programme.title: Stan Can
programme.desc: Stanley helps the kids play their big football match in all weather conditions, escalating into his latest creation, a high tech stadium!

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: When they land on the planet of Fairy Tales, the Fairy Tales meet two alien children. They immediately want to play with their new friends, but they are so sleepy that they can hardly keep their eyes open.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: When Ginger and the Pea-Rexes notice all the animals being pranked in the valley, they follow a series of clues to try to discover the culprit.

programme.title: Waffle's After School Club
programme.desc: When a smelly, dirty Waffle runs away at the mention of baths, everyone must find and clean him in time for him to lead a marching band sing along for their head teacher, Mr Nolan.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: Spring is the time of year when lots of different plants grow. Abbie and James join Aaron in the garden for a fun game of big plant, small plant, taking it in turns to spot plants of different sizes. They soon find a tiny one that's just poked its head above the soil - but where has it come from?

programme.title: Talking Tom Heroes: Suddenly Super
programme.desc: Hank loses a pile of his idea orbs, and they cause trouble all around the city.

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers learn how butterfly feeders can provide a source of energy for butterflies. They spend time designing their own using overripe fruit to help the butterflies in the garden.

programme.title: Andy's Dino Island
programme.desc: When Jen's drone goes missing on Dino Island, Andy heads out on an epic adventure to track it down. He discovers one of the island's inhabitants, a giraffe-sized pterosaur called Hatzegopteryx, is using the drone for a very special purpose. Along the way, he meets a herd of stampeding triceratops.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea's parents try to teach her to not lose her temper so she can beat Supertato.

programme.title: Magic Hands

programme.desc: The caravan is visited by a little owl learning to fly. The owl and Alva, Nick's granddaughter, give Nick the perfect idea for the song of the day.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard

programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo are playing The Quiet Game and won't use words to tell Dad which toy to buy. At his wits end, Dad recruits new store clerk Alfie to help interpret the miming.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa takes Goldie the Goldfish for a visit to the vets and is surprised when she finds that there's a new road to be built over the building! With the help of Mr. Bull and his brother they find that instead of demolishing the vets they can build a tunnel underneath it.

programme.title: Ben & Holly's Little Kingdom
programme.desc: When Redbeard, who is giving Nanny Plum, Ben and Holly a ride on his `unsinkable' boat, hits a rock, he calls on the services of Elf Rescue.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends: All Engines Go
programme.desc: When Thomas spends the day carrying rockstar idol Harold across Sodor, he gains a new perspective on just how amazing it is to be his engine-self.

programme.title: Fireman Sam
programme.desc: Charlie is having a day at the beach with James and Sarah. But today is not Charlie's day. It seems that everything he does turns into a disaster and makes him look uncool.

programme.title: Tim Rex In Space
programme.desc: Tim and Kai attempt to take a photograph of the elusive SpaceBug.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends learn how to animate their drawings at playgroup.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mum, Dad, Peppa and George decide to go to the beach on a beautiful sunny day. Suddenly, a little cloud covers the sun for a minute, but then it passes and the sun reappears.

programme.title: Pip and Posy
programme.desc: When Pip picks Zac to be in his team for the wheelbarrow race, Posy is upset and overcompensates when playing with Jamila. As they are too proud to make up, Frankie finds a clever way to bring them back together.

programme.title: The Woohoos!
programme.desc: Scotty is afraid of thunder and his friends want to help him. They work out that you can tell how close or far away a storm is by counting the time between lightning and thunder.

programme.title: The Adventures of Paddington
programme.desc: Mrs. Bird decides to train Paddington for the annual fun run in Windsor Gardens Park.

programme.title: PAW Patrol

programme.desc: Rubble leads the pups in building an emergency landing pad for Captain Gordy in this Ultimate Construction Rescue!

programme.title: Animal Care Club

programme.title: The Creature Cases
programme.desc: Friendly rhinos gather at the Watering Hole.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob loses the use of his thumbs, and must get them back in working order before his entire life falls apart.

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun: Cutdowns

programme.title: Susan Calman's Grand Days Out
programme.desc: In Kent, Susan visits Hever Castle, the childhood home of Anne Boleyn, Canterbury Cathedral, the premier place for a medieval pilgrimage, and Penshurst, where she tries her hand at fencing.

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben travels to the Sierra Nevada mountains in the USA to meet Mike Basich, a snowboarder who left professional sport to reconnect with nature. Ben joins him in his hand-built log cabin and follows him as he embarks on a life changing project.

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben Fogle returns to the Arctic circle to revisit old friend and fellow adventurer Randi Skaug living on her remote island off Norway's northern coastline. He finds out how the intermittent years have treated Randi.

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: In Cheltenham, a pair of pups are making focusing on driving impossible. Dawn's Yorkie, Teddy, gives the clients a hair-raising send off every time they try to leave. Chris and Ken's Collie Mollie's reaction to phone calls has beached their peace.

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: A green-eyed Whippet Clyde disrupts any attempt at affection between owners Claire and Steve. Dance instructors Sam and Clive's Bartholemew is leaving them at risk of breaking a leg. Romanian rescue, Kevin, still cannot feel at home.

programme.title: Catching Up with the Royals With Rev Richard Coles
programme.desc: Reverend Richard Coles and royal correspondent Emily Andrews delve beyond the pomp and pageantry of royal life, revealing what's really going on behind the headlines of the world's most famous family - the Windsors.

programme.title: Jumanji
programme.desc: Alan Parrish disappeared as a young boy. Nobody believes his friend's story that he was sucked into the game they were playing. Twenty-six years later, two children find the game in their attic, and when they start to play, Alan is released, but the game isn't over yet, and Alan must finish before he is really free.

programme.desc: A young lawyer joins a small but prestigious law firm only to find out that most of their clients are on the wrong side of the law. programme.desc: Vinod Joshi lives a middle-class existence with his wife, Meena, and a young son. Their well being is shattered by the arrival of a letter from an ex-employee, alleging that he saw Vinod committing a murder. Meena initially dismisses it as absurd, but one day Vinod returns home in a bloodied state telling there has been a homicide. She attempts to trace the origin of this wealth and gets more and more convinced about his guilt.

programme.title: Chaand Ka Tukda
programme.desc: A rich NRI comes to India in search of a bride, only to fall in love with a village girl. However, she just might be a small pawn in a bigger, deadlier conspiracy.

programme.sub-title: The One With All the Haste

programme.desc: Ross tells Rachel that he cannot be around her once Emily comes to New York. Meanwhile, Chandler and Monica try to have a romantic weekend.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh embark on a 3,000-mile journey through Europe, starting off in the sunny south of Croatia and ending up in the ice-cold Arctic Circle in Scandinavia. programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: They plan to travel from their home in Essex all the way to the Amalfi Coast in Italy, but the first stop on their road trip is the Netherlands, where they try out one of the country's oldest sports: canal jumping. The action continues when the pair don Lycra for a spot of velodrome track cycling alongside a world champion.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh continue their European adventure in Germany. They head to the city of Frankfurt, where Brad is literally pushed to the edge as he attempts to abseil down the side of a building, facing forwards. The boys also make a stop at a bratwurst-themed hotel, and soak up some local Bavarian culture when they have a go at finger wrestling. The pair finish off their time in Germany with a wet and wild adventure in the mountains.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The next stop on Bradley and Barney Walsh's European road trip is Switzerland. They take in the breath-taking views with a ride on a mountain rollercoaster, before exercising their vocal chords when they try the Swiss tradition of yodeling. The following day, Barney arranges a unique way for them to enjoy some local cheese, and then they drive to the Contra Dam, where Barney reveals they are about to attempt one of James Bond's most iconic stunts: a petrifying 220 metre-high bungee jump.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The father and son have reached Slovenia, a country that loves its sport. Brad's passion for bikes gives Barney an excuse to take his dad motocross racing. After hearing that one in 200 people in Slovenia is a beekeeper, they make a stop to meet one.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: For the penultimate leg of their journey Bradley and Barney head to Italy, starting by the picturesque Lake Garda, where Barney surprises his dad by explaining they will be running off the top of Monte Baldo and paragliding over the lake below. Soon after, Bradley takes a dip in the lake when he tries paddleboard yoga. The next day the guys travel to the River Po for a boat trip like no other, as Bradley is treated to a hair-raising ride in a Formula 1 speed boat.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Barney and Bradley Walsh embark on the final leg of their European road trip as they continue to explore Italy. Having reached Rome, they take a trip back in time to battle it out as gladiators, before continuing south and making a stop to soak up the views from a sky-high zipwire. As the boys reach their final stop on the Amalfi Coast, they get the chance to swim in the stunning sea, but they have to cliff-dive in first.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney's epic journey continues north, having left Croatia behind they're saying hello to Hungary, where the boys take to the open waters of Lake Balaton for a spot of high-speed catamaran sailing with an Olympic crew, before warming up in the RV with some world-famous Goulash. The pair finish their time in Hugary's capital Budapest, where Barney's arranged to meet a world-famous escapologist.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney continue their European adventure in Poland. The boys take in a spot of sightseeing in Krakow, before they don wetsuits and head to a freezing cold lake to try out water blobbing. Next up, they come face to face with man's best friend as Brad gets chased by an attack dog. All before Barney treats his dad to an exclusive performance with the Polish National Radio Symphony Orchestra in honour of Poland's most famous composer, Chopin.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney arrive in Denmark for the Scandinavian leg of their tour. Bradley's fear of heights is pushed to the limit with a visit to an offshore wind turbine, and the boys test both of their boundaries at world's tallest indoor rope course.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: A florist gets the surprise of her life when she becomes the unexpected star of the show, and former Welsh international footballer Robbie Savage hands over his phone for another celebrity edition of Send to All.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: After everyone sees Robert and Amy having sex, they move into Ray's basement office to enjoy some privacy.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra prepare Ally for her first play date and reflect on their own first meeting and kiss.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: After a PTA meeting Debra makes a comment about a mother who had a breast enhancement and gets upset after seeing Ray's reaction. When Ray returns home from a trip, Debra has a "big" surprise for him.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra have an argument about their new can opener, and Marie, Frank and Robert can't help getting involved.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: After an accidental tip-off from Ray which allows him to win a bet, Frank makes everyone suspicious when he begins to show an unusual interest in Ray's work.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra try to decide who should care for their children in case anything should happen to them.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with All the Thanksgivings
programme.desc: As the gang digests Monica's huge Thanksgiving meal, everyone reminisces about their worst Thanksgiving ever.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Warning notes on Ross's lunch causes concern among his co-workers, and Phoebe takes a literature class.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Inappropriate Sister
programme.desc: After returning from another disappointing audition, Joey tries to write a play in which he can star. Meanwhile, Rachel gets a date with her neighbour Danny, but is perturbed to discover his extremely intimate relationship with his sexy sister.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Resolutions
programme.desc: The friends make their New Year resolutions, and Joey tries to arrange for Chandler to kiss Monica at midnight at a New Year's Eve party.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Chandler's Work Laugh
programme.desc: Monica is annoyed when she discovers Chandler is ingratiating himself with his new boss Doug. He even has a fake laugh which he uses whenever Doug cracks a joke. Meanwhile, Rachel is obsessed with finding out the gossip between Monica and Chandler after overhearing a sexy conversation between them, and Ross astounds everyone with his new girlfriend, a blast from the past.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: At her grandmother's funeral, Phoebe is astounded to meet her biological father, Frank Sr, who she has been trying to find for years. Elsewhere, Chandler tells perfectionist Monica that she is useless at massage, and Joey's fondness for an expensive bag loses him an audition.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Everybody Finds Out
programme.desc: Chandler and Monica's relationship becomes less of a secret, and Ross must prove to his boss that he's sane in order to return to work.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Girl Who Hits Joey
programme.desc: Chandler becomes anxious when he thinks Monica wants more from their relationship, and Ross moves into his new flat and fails to impress his new neighbours. Meanwhile, Joey is afraid to end his relationship with a diminutive woman who punches him hard every time she thinks something is fun.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe finds a police officer's badge and goes on a spree of arrests until she meets up with the owner, while Joey wonders if he is in love with Monica.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachel's Inadvertent Kiss
programme.desc: Rachel has an interview for her dream job at Ralph Lauren. All goes well until she inadvertently kisses the interviewer, and at the second interview she mistakenly berates him for his `sexual intentions'. Meanwhile, Monica competes to have a better relationship than Phoebe's, and Joey is arrested for stalking a sexy neighbour with whom Ross gets a date.

programme.desc: Deputies are assaulted during a hearing; courtroom chaos after a sentencing; a murderer's wife turns state's witness.

programme.title: Court Cam

programme.title: My Strange Arrest

programme.title: My Strange Arrest
programme.desc: A man decides to interrupt his crime to make a pizza; police track down a man who jumped through the roof of a port-a-potty; an unwanted intruder is arrested after he fell asleep in the middle of a crime.

programme.title: My Strange Arrest
